description_long: This is an elongated, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly gray coloration and subtle variations in tone across its surface. The stone features a rough, uneven texture with visible indentations and natural contours, suggesting it has been shaped by weathering or manual chipping. One side displays a faint, hand-written alphanumeric marking in white, likely for identification or cataloging purposes. The overall form is tapered at both ends, with the central section being the widest and thickest. The surface finish is matte and non-reflective, with no visible polish or artificial treatment. The stone appears solid and dense, typical of natural rocks found in urban landscaping or as obstacles in parks and sidewalks.
description: An elongated, rough-textured gray stone with tapered ends and a matte, uneven surface, featuring a white alphanumeric marking on one side.
description_view_0: A narrow, edge-on view showing the stone's thin profile and subtle surface undulations.
description_view_1: A broad side view revealing the full length, rough surface, and visible white alphanumeric marking.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view, similar to view 0, highlighting the stone's slenderness and natural contours.
description_view_3: The opposite broad side, showing the stone's irregular surface without visible markings.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle natural mottling and irregular surface indentations, with a hand-written alphanumeric marking on one side.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with signs of natural weathering but no significant erosion or biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts moderately with paved or grassy urban environments, often partially embedded in soil or surrounded by smaller debris.
